title: Kaworus Gameplan
wikitext:
[]: Kaworu's Gameplan             Quote Post by Lye » Tue Jul 23, 2013 11:41 pm One thing that I've always been confused about is was Kaworu always planning on betraying SEELE from the moment (or before) he met Shinji, or did he only decide to do so when he broke into Central Dogma and found out Adam was really Lilith? If Kaworu had always planned on betraying SEELE and sacrificing himself from the very beginning, then why did he cut it so close to the bone? Why work so hard on incapacitating NERV and hurting Shinji and go all the way down to Central Dogma as if he was actually going to cause Third Impact? On the other hand, if Kaworu only made the decision to sacrifice himself in Central Dogma, does it mean that if the figure in Dogma turned out to be Adam like he originally thought, he would have merged with it and eradicated humanity? tl;dr KAWORU IS A CONFUSING MESS EPISODE 24 CONFUSES ME GREATLY

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Wed Jul 24, 2013 12:30 am If you're willing to read it, there's this long thread which tries to reconcile all of the nonsense from episode 24: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2095/The-Great-Episode-24-Plot-Hole-Fanwank-Thread/ More: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/11183/Filling-the-24-Plot-Hole-Once-and-for-All/ http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/3810/Kaworus-Purpose-Nature/ (A really old archived thread from Anime Nation)

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Wed Jul 24, 2013 1:03 pm Ornette wrote: If you're willing to read it, there's this long thread which tries to reconcile all of the nonsense from episode 24: http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/2095/The-Great-Episode-24-Plot-Hole-Fanwank-Thread/

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 26, 2013 4:59 pm Bagheera wrote: Erm. Ornette, why aren't your theories from the first couple of pages in this thread on the wiki page for that episode? You hit the nail square on the head and essentially ended the matter some six and a half years ago. No more plot hole, no need for the Casablanca theory, no more problems. Just Kaworu saying "man, what the fuck is going on?" and rambling a bit as he tries to sort things out.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jul 26, 2013 5:23 pm Ornette wrote: To be honest, it was sort of a "for fun" and not serious thing at first, but then there was more and more discussion about it. Also, I think NAveryW came up with something similar independently at some point. It's still got some issues with it and I've stopped putting effort into it long ago.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Tue Nov 12, 2013 2:53 pm Ok, let's see if I can wrap this up. I'm not trying to take any merit of authorship, but rather I am double checking with you guys whether I got it right and whether I can summarise it well enough and concisely enough to maybe make it the Wiki? Let's see if my approach (situation-target-path) works… SEELE'S SITUATION/PROBLEM AT THE TIME OF EP 24 - Eva-01 has become an absolute being at Gendo's disposal and can pretty much be piloted only by Shinji as we see in Ep 19. Ep 21': SEELE 02: With an S2 engine on board, Evangelion Unit 01 has become an absolute being! SEELE 03: We have no use for an actualized god. KEEL: We must not create a god. SEELE ??: We cannot allow that man to create a god. Gendo Ikari, is he a man that can be trusted?

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Nov 26, 2013 9:07 pm Aaagh, Falcon_of_the_Sun, this theory reminds of the wasted hours and ruined hopes I've spent trying to fill in this pothole! But since I've done it so often I can think of a few recurring responses. Primarily, this a "Seele betrays kaworu (by getting him killed)"-type thesis, and their common problem is this: Sending him to NERV risks a hostile impact, Kaworu's capture, the self-destruction of the geofront and all its Impact materials, for the potential upside of collateral damage and killing Kaworu. Further, why would they have him kept alive all this time if they want him to die? People's responses have included "they need Gendou's marvellous machines to kill an angel and he won't do it unless they force his hand" and "keeping him alive gives them control over the timeline", respectively. Also, the first time I heard Seele's "we must no longer rely on Adam" line I instantly assumed it meant they had been relying on Kaworu in some way, and they perceived him as betraying them. However, I'm told that this is only in the Platinum edition's translation, and other editions portray it wildly differently (and I'm reminded it's technically ambiguous.) Consequently, I tend towards a "Kaworu betrays Seele, (by going rogue)"-model, but the a Seele "Hehe, one dead Kaworu and free traumatized Shinji, too" ending does have some obvious advantages. - What I would like to add to this discussion, though, is the Episode 24 Draft scripts that fandom have discovered. Monk Ed is doing a big thing about the F. o' K. on another thread, and the real problem with S. o' L. discussions is Episode 24' plays a big role in their implied mechanics/plot utilisation, and it's hard to solve one without the other. I don't know if they laughably debunked/dubious forgeries, so forget this if they are. That could be why there don't seem to be any pages that link to them in the wiki, but in terms of implied 24plot/SoL mechanics they have some key features. Particularly since MonkEd's theory involves Unit-02 in an important way, I don't know if I should post this there, but here goes: 1. An important consistency between 24d1, 24d2, and ep. 24 is Kaworu breaks into Terminal Dogma and nearly starts the Third Impact. (Some "Seele betrays Kaworu"-theories have suggested he can't, and likewise some S. o' L. concepts.) 2. NOT consistent is Unit-02. In one draft he pilots it, in another Asuka fails to start it and he transforms into Tabris. 3. The scripts in the drafts identifies the creature contained in Terminal Dogma as 'Adam'. Kaworu's reasons for rejecting the Third Impact are not based on encountering Lilith. edit: and they're here, I should say, http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:Episode_24_Draft_1_(Translation) http://wiki.evageeks.org/Resources:Episode_24_Draft_2_(Translation)

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Nov 27, 2013 3:49 am Thanks for the input! I'd never call Seele's behaviour "a betrayal" of Kaworu. Seele knew from day 1 that Kaworu was an Angel, an enemy. Betrayal would imply some previous, bona fide partnership, which was never there, because the plan was always to destroy all Angels. But again, semantics, not that important... With regard to "why they didn't kill him?" the answer might be as simple as "they could not". Kaworu's powers seem immense (greatest AT Field ever record until Rei III does the same thing). It looks like that unless Kaworu truly allowed himself to be killed, even an Eva would have struggled to destroy him. He even brags about being destined to live forever and to basically have suicide as the only act of free will in an otherwise eternal existence... Also, Kaworu could not make an impact (of course, he thought he could because he was told so), because he can't do it with Lilith. Whether finding Gendo and re-obtaining Adam's sample would be enough for him to make an Impact, I don't know if there is evidence about this. All in all, I don't think Seele were risking loads in sending Kaworu to Nerv/TD. I wasn't reading MonkEd's other big topic of these days, but I might now that you say it contains relavant stuff. Thanks for the heads up. :)

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Wed Nov 27, 2013 6:26 am What we have here is a kind of matrix of problems, not so much a plot hole as a thicket of interlocking plans and implications in Eva's world. In particular, this discussion has gone around a few times, so when I posted that "well, if Seele sent Kaworu to NERV they must have a good reason to risk 3I". A common response has been, "kaworu's being tricked and for some reason can't do that" and so I should have remembered to put in my post is "but Gendo and Misato in ep. 24 very clearly think so" which is one of those things where the discussion goes around in a circle. With regards to the word "betrayal", that's because this glorious fandom has uncovered script work stating that this episode includes a "smile of betrayal" that appears in staff writings but not on the script as presented. The assumption seems to be that Kaworu's fey smile betrays someone, somewhere, so that' s why I categorise that way. In particular the quote "Our only choice is to make changes with our own hands for the future. I will be praying that Unit 01 will accomplish its task" follows "we must no rely on Adam and the Angels" and occurs during Kaworu's movement to Terminal Dogma. This somewhat suggests that Seele are spooked by what Kaworu is doing, and think for some reason he has "betrayed" them, while the context of Unit-01's pursuit more strongly suggests that means they do indeed plan betray and kill Kaworu, as you say. Given that I think we can be sure that a) Seele sent Kaworu, and b) they want him dead at some later point, and c) Kaworu risks 3I, and d) someone betrays someone in some way, a theory has to be built around these. That is, I think they must definitely be risking something, so surely they must have something to gain. I think the dominant responses are they're trying to persuade Kaworu to kill Gendo or turn Shinji or something because their one clear goal, the death of Kaworu should surely be easier to achieve. Unless, as you say, they can't do that without Gendo. The problem is syncing it with the implied fruit mechanics of, say EoE. That's why I say there's this thicket of what Kaworu wants, what Seele wants, and what they could reasonably achieve with the given materials, all which stretches from 24 on to EoE. For example, in the earlier plothole thread someone pointed out Seele say in Eoe: "the Lance of Longinus now lost, complementation using Lilith is impossible. Our only hope is to proceed with EVA-01, Lilith's alter ego." I don't know what to do with that, frankly.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Wed Nov 27, 2013 2:04 pm I strongly disagree about the risks of Kaworu making an Impact. When you say "Gendo and Misato", I'm afraid that's at least incorrect. Misato is under the belief that they have Adam down there. Gendo and Fuyu, who know the truth, don't even finch at the idea of Kaworu approaching the crucifixed giant. I guess the viewer might even sniff that, if there was a real risk, it would be Gendo, not Misato to mention self-destructing the whole Nerv HQ.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Nov 27, 2013 8:54 pm SoryuUberAlles wrote: The assumption seems to be that Kaworu's fey smile betrays someone, somewhere, so that' s why I categorise that way. In particular the quote "Our only choice is to make changes with our own hands for the future. I will be praying that Unit 01 will accomplish its task" follows "we must no rely on Adam and the Angels" and occurs during Kaworu's movement to Terminal Dogma. This somewhat suggests that Seele are spooked by what Kaworu is doing, and think for some reason he has "betrayed" them, while the context of Unit-01's pursuit more strongly suggests that means they do indeed plan betray and kill Kaworu, as you say.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Wed Nov 27, 2013 9:41 pm I wonder if poor Reichu ever thought for a moment, when "debating" with this Eva Yojimbo character, deep in her subconscious: "Not sure if trolling or being trolled" Man, I'll say it again, it's disappointing that I didn't know about this forums existence before. The shit that I missed out on.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Wed Nov 27, 2013 9:47 pm That thread was my grand entrance to the forum .

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Thu Nov 28, 2013 5:13 am Monk Ed, thanks for the OA dialogue. That "it's all according to plan" pretty much says it all. To me, that's a wrap. Again, not trying to take any merits here, I have just collated together and organised (in what I believe is a good, orderly manner) the results of other people's reasoning. But I think that's it. Seele's agenda was to get Kaworu killed, hoping to get a traumatised/cathatonic Shinji and a Nerv with one Eva less (02) in the process, so that the EoE Seele impact would have been all the easier to carry out. Kaworu was made believe that Seele wanted an Angel impact to stop Gendo and allow the true successors from the White Moon to get what was theirs from the start. He then changed his mind and decided to get killed through a combination of 1 - him liking Shinji 2 - the realisation that Seele/Lilin were clever enough to trick him into chasing Adam which was in fact Lilith, thus possibly proving themselves as worthy successors in the evolutionary battle. Basically, the apparently "non-sensical Lilin" finally make sense to Kaworu. 3 - existential ennui as it dawned on him that if he couldn't impact, he would have had to live forever, so he might as well enjoy some free will and elect to die.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Nov 28, 2013 5:51 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: Seele's agenda was to get Kaworu killed, hoping to get a traumatised/cathatonic Shinji and a Nerv with one Eva less (02) in the process, so that the EoE Seele impact would have been all the easier to carry out.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Thu Nov 28, 2013 9:08 am Ok, yes, it makes sense that the traumatisation of Shinji was not a deliberate objective of Seele, but rather an unexpected yet kinda welcome byproduct of Kaworu's coming. With regard to the SDSS, I think that what is called "the script" and the SDSS are two different things. The SDSS are indeed an instruction manual of sorts for things the FAR created (source: CI). The script is the plan that Seele has devised and will follow given the knowledge and instructions taken from the SDSS. Only once it looks like they are considered one and the same, see below Gendo Ep 14. Some more instances below. However, although Ep 23 DC clearly says MAN C (OFF): At last, we have defeated up through the 16th Angel. MAN D (OFF): That leaves only one Angel described in Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls. the fact that the SDSS contemplated the existence of a 17th Angel is no guarantee that it also detailed what his mission was meant to be, if any. Also, there is a chance that Seele has never disclosed the full (S?)DDS to Gendo&Fuyu, but rather created the script exactly to allow them to work things on Seele's behalf. 24 DC SOME SEELE MEMBER: Nerv, the organization formed to be Seele's executive branch... SOME SEELE MEMBER: It was created in order to put our script into practice. 14 KEEL (OFF): This incident deviated slightly from our script. IKARI (OFF): Nevertheless, the result was within the projected parameters. We can make the necessary adjustments. IKARI: The time schedule is advancing as described in the Dead Sea Scrolls. KEEL: Fine then. We'll leave aside the matter of crimes or responsibility on your part. But there is no need for you to create a new script. IKARI: I realize that. Everything is proceeding according to Seele's script. IKARI: Everything is going according to our script. There is no problem. FUYUTSUKI: What about the Unit 00 accident? That wasn't in my script. 19 Kaji: Seele won't stay quiet for this. Was this also part of your script, Commander Ikari? 20 COMMITTEEMAN ?: This incident is at extreme odds with our script. COMMITTEEMAN ?: It will not be easy to correct for this.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Nov 28, 2013 12:34 pm Monk Ed wrote: Alternatively (and perhaps more likely), it might have been to satisfy a requirement spelled out in the SDSS for whatever reason: Consider that although Gendo and Fuyutsuki knew nothing of Kaworu and were surprised when he turned out to be an Angel, they knew that the next Angel would be the last . This has some pretty spectacular implications when you think about it, because it suggests that Kaworu's existence (or at least, the role he was to fill) was spelled out in the SDSS as the official final Angel according to it, yet it was not spelled out in a way that would give Gendo or Fuyutsuki the heads-up (like, it obviously didn't say "create the final Angel yourselves", Seele apparently decided to do that all on their own). I should note that I actually just now noticed this as I was typing this post, and now that I realize it I must ponder what this implies.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Thu Nov 28, 2013 8:55 pm I'm not sure you read my post right if that's how you're responding, but, regardless, regarding the point about Tabris not being part of the plan because he wasn't made by Adam, look at the post above yours: Seele, episode 23 wrote: ?? (OFF): At last, we have defeated up through the 16th Angel. ?? (OFF): That leaves only one Angel described in Seele's Dead Sea Scrolls.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Fri Nov 29, 2013 3:36 am Although I tend to agree with you MonkEd, the mentioning of a 17th Angel possibly named Tabris (under the assumption that all the other names were specified by the SDSS and not made up by Seele feat. jewish mysticism) in the SDSS does not mean that the SDSS also specify that it was going to look like a Lilin and to do the stuff he ends up doing. Although the word "described" definitely is a bit more than "listed", but I reckon it could be a translation thing... This said, I believe Bagheera was trying to reinfornce the concept that the SDSS are an instruction manual, a book of recipes for FAR technology. The script is more like the planned menu of the fancy dinner, given those FAR dishes. Kinda...

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Fri Nov 29, 2013 6:35 am Falcon_of_the_Sun wrote: Although I tend to agree with you MonkEd, the mentioning of a 17th Angel possibly named Tabris (under the assumption that all the other names were specified by the SDSS and not made up by Seele feat. jewish mysticism) in the SDSS does not mean that the SDSS also specify that it was going to look like a Lilin and to do the stuff he ends up doing. Although the word "described" definitely is a bit more than "listed", but I reckon it could be a translation thing...

Article: Kaworus Gameplan
[]: Kaworu's Gameplan             Quote Post by Lye » Tue Jul 23, 2013 11:41 pm One thing that I've always been confused about is was Kaworu always planning on betraying SEELE from the moment (or before) he met Shinji, or did he only decide to do so when he broke into Central Dogma and found out Adam was really Lilith? If Kaworu had always planned on betraying SEELE and sacrificing himself from the very beginning, then why did he cut it so close to the bone? Why work so hard on incapacitating NERV and hurting Shinji and go all the way down to Central Dogma as if he was actually going to cause Third Impact? On the other hand, if Kaworu only made the decision to sacrifice himself in Central Dogma, does it mean that if the figure in Dogma turned out to be Adam like he originally thought, he would have merged with it and eradicated humanity? tl;dr KAWORU IS A CONFUSING MESS EPISODE 24 CONFUSES ME GREATLY

Seele's agenda was to get Kaworu killed, hoping to get a traumatised/cathatonic Shinji and a Nerv with one Eva less (02) in the process, so that the EoE Seele impact would have been all the easier to carry out. Kaworu was made believe that Seele wanted an Angel impact to stop Gendo and allow the true successors from the White Moon to get what was theirs from the start.
